title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct security threat but could become a target for exploitation if improperly configured, used on sensitive systems, or if malicious actors tamper with its codebase. Additionally, reliance on such tools without proper validation may lead to false positives or negatives.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and ensure its integrity before deployment.
Use the tool in conjunction with other security measures to validate findings and avoid over-reliance.
Monitor the tool's repository for updates, patches, or reported vulnerabilities.

title: A vulnerability was identified in Nothings stb up to 1.22. The impacted element is the function...
description: A vulnerability was identified in Nothings stb up to 1.22. The impacted element is the function setup_free of the file stb_vorbis.c. The manipulation leads to allocation of resources. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5316.

title: A vulnerability was determined in Nothings stb up to 1.26. The affected element is the function...
description: A vulnerability was determined in Nothings stb up to 1.26. The affected element is the function stbtt__buf_get8 in the library stb_truetype.h of the component TTF File Handler. Executing a manipulation can lead to out-of-bounds read. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5315.

title: HCL BigFix Platform is affected by insecure permissions on private cryptographic keys.  The...
description: HCL BigFix Platform is affected by insecure permissions on private cryptographic keys.  The private cryptographic keys located on a Windows host machine might be subject to overly permissive file system permissions.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The insecure permissions on private cryptographic keys in the HCL BigFix Platform could allow unauthorized users or malicious actors to access and potentially misuse these keys. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized system access, or compromise of encrypted communications.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and restrict file system permissions on private cryptographic keys to ensure only authorized users have access.
Apply any available patches or updates provided by HCL to address this vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit to identify and remediate any other instances of overly permissive file system permissions.

title: A vulnerability was found in Nothings stb up to 1.26. Impacted is the function...

Performing a manipulation results in out-of-bounds read. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: V-SFT versions 6.2.10.0 and prior contain an out-of-bounds read in VS6ComFile!get_macro_mem_COM....
description: V-SFT versions 6.2.10.0 and prior contain an out-of-bounds read in VS6ComFile!get_macro_mem_COM. Opening a crafted V7 file may lead to information disclosure from the affected product.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in V-SFT versions 6.2.10.0 and prior allows an attacker to exploit an out-of-bounds read by opening a crafted V7 file, potentially leading to information disclosure. This could expose sensitive data or system configurations to unauthorized parties.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of V-SFT software that addresses CVE-2026-32929 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strict file validation and only open V7 files from trusted sources to minimize the risk of exploitation.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts and apply additional network segmentation to isolate vulnerable systems.

title: IBM Aspera Shares 1.9.9 through 1.11.0 is vulnerable to HTTP header injection, caused by improper...
description: IBM Aspera Shares 1.9.9 through 1.11.0 is vulnerable to HTTP header injection, caused by improper validation of input by the HOST headers.  This could allow an attacker to conduct various attacks against the vulnerable system, including cross-site scripting, cache poisoning or session hijacking.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in IBM Aspera Shares could allow attackers to exploit improper validation of HTTP HOST headers, leading to potential cross-site scripting (XSS), cache poisoning, or session hijacking. This could compromise data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.
recommended_actions: Upgrade IBM Aspera Shares to a version later than 1.11.0, where the vulnerability has been patched.
Implement input validation and sanitization for HTTP headers at the application or proxy level to mitigate potential exploitation.
Monitor logs and network traffic for unusual activity, such as unexpected HTTP header manipulations, to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Nothings stb up to 2.30. This issue affects the function...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Nothings stb up to 2.30. This issue affects the function stbi__gif_load_next in the library stb_image.h of the component GIF Decoder. Such manipulation leads to denial of service. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the stb_image.h library's GIF decoder function (stbi__gif_load_next) can lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) attack. This could cause applications relying on the library to crash or become unresponsive, potentially disrupting services that process user-supplied GIF images.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and update to the latest version of the stb library if a patch is available. If no patch exists, consider replacing or disabling the vulnerable GIF decoding functionality.
Implement input validation and sanitization to ensure that only trusted or verified GIF files are processed by the application.
Monitor application logs and network traffic for any signs of exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.

title: A path traversal vulnerability in the Fireware OS Web UI on WatchGuard Firebox systems may allow...
description: A path traversal vulnerability in the Fireware OS Web UI on WatchGuard Firebox systems may allow a privileged authenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code in the context of an elevated system process.This issue affects Fireware OS 12.6.1 up to and including 12.11.8 and 2025.1 up to and including 2026.1.2.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3987.

title: IBM DataPower Gateway 10.6CD 10.6.1.0 through 10.6.5.0 and IBM DataPower Gateway 10.5.0 10.5.0.0...
description: IBM DataPower Gateway 10.6CD 10.6.1.0 through 10.6.5.0 and IBM DataPower Gateway 10.5.0 10.5.0.0 through 10.5.0.20 and IBM DataPower Gateway 10.6.0 10.6.0.0 through 10.6.0.8 IBM DataPower Gateway is vulnerable to cross-site request forgery which could allow an attacker to execute malicious and unauthorized actions transmitted from a user that the website trusts.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit cross-site request forgery (CSRF), potentially enabling unauthorized actions on trusted websites. This could lead to data manipulation, unauthorized access, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Update IBM DataPower Gateway to the latest patched version to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement CSRF protection mechanisms, such as anti-CSRF tokens, to prevent exploitation.
Conduct a security audit to identify and address any additional vulnerabilities in the affected systems.

title: CVE-2026-4826 - A vulnerability was determined in SourceCodester Sales and Inventory System 1.0. This vulnerability
description: A vulnerability was determined in SourceCodester Sales and Inventory System 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /update_stock.php of the component HTTP GET Parameter Handler. This manipulation of the argument sid causes sql injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4826.

title: CVE-2026-4758 - The WP Job Portal plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to insufficient
description: The WP Job Portal plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to insufficient file path validation in the 'WPJOBPORTALcustomfields::removeFileCustom' function in all versions up to, and including, 2.4.9. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to delete arbitrary files on the server, which can easily lead to remote code execution when the right file is deleted (such as wp-config.php).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4758.

title: CVE-2026-34056 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. A Broken Access Control vulnerability in OpenEMR up to and including version 8.0.0.3 allows low-privilege users to view and download Ensora eRx error logs without proper authorization checks. This flaw compromises system confidentiality by exposing sensitive information, potentially leading to unauthorized data disclosure and misuse. As of time of publication, no known patches versions are available.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in OpenEMR allows low-privilege users to access sensitive eRx error logs without proper authorization, potentially leading to the exposure of confidential patient data and misuse of sensitive information. This could result in regulatory non-compliance, reputational damage, and financial loss for affected organizations.
recommended_actions: Immediately restrict access to the affected components by implementing stricter access control policies and monitoring user activity.
Apply virtual patches or workarounds, such as custom access control rules, until an official patch is released by the OpenEMR development team.
Monitor OpenEMR's official communication channels for updates and apply the patch as soon as it becomes available.

title: CVE-2026-34055 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Prior to version 8.0.0.3, the legacy patient notes functions in `library/pnotes.inc.php` perform updates and deletes using `WHERE id = ?` without verifying that the note belongs to a patient the user is authorized to access. Multiple web UI callers pass user-controlled note IDs directly to these functions. This is the same class of vulnerability as CVE-2026-25745 (REST API IDOR), but affects the web UI code paths. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34055.

title: CVE-2026-34053 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Prior to version 8.0.0.3, missing authorization in the AJAX deletion endpoint `interface/forms/procedure_order/handle_deletions.php` allows any authenticated user, regardless of role, to irreversibly delete procedure orders, answers, and specimens belonging to any patient in the system. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34053.

title: CVE-2026-34051 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Versions prior to 8.0.0.3 have an improper access control on the Import/Export functionality, allowing unauthorized users to perform import and export actions through direct request manipulation despite UI restrictions. This can lead to unauthorized data access, bulk data extraction, and manipulation of system data. Version 8.0.0.3 contains a fix.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34051.

title: CVE-2026-33934 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Versions prior to 8.0.0.3 have a missing authorization check in `portal/sign/lib/show-signature.php` that allows any authenticated patient portal user to retrieve the drawn signature image of any staff member by supplying an arbitrary `user` value in the POST body. The companion write endpoint (`save-signature.php`) was already hardened against this same issue, but the read endpoint was not updated to match. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33934.

title: CVE-2026-33933 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Starting in version 7.0.2.1 and prior to version 8.0.0.3, a reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the custom template editor allows an attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript in an authenticated staff member's browser session by sending them a crafted URL. The attacker does not need an OpenEMR account. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33933.

title: CVE-2026-33932 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Prior to version 8.0.0.3, a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in the CCDA document preview allows an attacker who can upload or send a CCDA document to execute arbitrary JavaScript in a clinician's browser session when the document is previewed. The XSL stylesheet sanitizes attributes for all other narrative elements but not for `linkHtml`, allowing `href="javascript:..."` and event handler attributes to pass through unchanged. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33932.

title: CVE-2026-33931 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Prior to version 8.0.0.3, an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerability in the patient portal payment page allows any authenticated portal patient to access other patients' payment records — including invoice/billing data (PHI) and payment card metadata — by manipulating the `recid` query parameter in `portal/portal_payment.php`. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33931.

title: CVE-2026-33918 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Prior to version 8.0.0.3, the billing file-download endpoint `interface/billing/get_claim_file.php` only verifies that the caller has a valid session and CSRF token, but does not check any ACL permissions. This allows any authenticated OpenEMR user — regardless of whether they have billing privileges — to download and permanently delete electronic claim batch files containing protected health information (PHI). Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33918.

title: CVE-2026-33917 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Versions prior to 8.0.0.3 contais a SQL injection vulnerability in the ajax_save CAMOS form that can be exploited by authenticated attackers. The vulnerability exists due to insufficient input validation in the ajax_save page in the CAMOS form. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33917.

title: CVE-2026-33915 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Prior to version 8.0.0.3, five insurance company REST API routes are missing the `RestConfig::request_authorization_check()` call that every other data-modifying route in the standard API uses. This allows any authenticated API user to create and modify insurance company records even if their OpenEMR user account does not have administrative ACL permissions. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33915.

title: CVE-2026-33914 - OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management applicat
description: OpenEMR is a free and open source electronic health records and medical practice management application. Prior to version 8.0.0.3, the PostCalendar module contains a blind SQL injection vulnerability in the `categoriesUpdate` administrative function. The `dels` POST parameter is read via `pnVarCleanFromInput()`, which only strips HTML tags and performs no SQL escaping. The value is then interpolated directly into a raw SQL `DELETE` statement that is executed unsanitized via Doctrine DBAL's `executeStatement()`. Version 8.0.0.3 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33914.

title: CVE-2026-30892 - crun is an open source OCI Container Runtime fully written in C. In versions 1.19 through 1.26, the
description: crun is an open source OCI Container Runtime fully written in C. In versions 1.19 through 1.26, the  `crun exec` option `-u` (`--user`) is incorrectly parsed. The value `1` is interpreted as UID 0 and GID 0 when it should have been UID 1 and  GID 0. The process thus runs with higher privileges than expected. Version 1.27 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with none severity. CVE: CVE-2026-30892.
